name: U.s. Mints
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------GLAZE-----------------------------------
6 tablespoons Mint chocolate chips
1 ounce Unsweetened chocolate (finely chopped)
5 tablespoons Unsalted butter (cut into pieces)
FOR THE BROWNIES: Position the rack in the lowest third of the oven and preheat to 350°F. Butter the sides of a 9-inch square baking pan with high sides. Fold a piece of foil (18x12 inches) in half crosswise and line the pan with it, allowing the foil to extend over two sides. Butter the foil. Melt the chocolates and butter together in a heavy medium saucepan over very low heat, stirring until smooth. Cool.
Sift the flour and salt into a small bowl. Mix the cream, espresso powder, and Crème de Menthe in a small cup. Using an electric mixer, beat the eggs and sugar in a large bowl until frothy. Fold in the coffee mixture and then the melted chocolate. Fold in the dry ingredients. Spread the batter evenly in the prepared pan. Bake until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out with some moist crumbs still attached, about 25 minutes (the surface may crack). Gently press down on any raised surfaces to flatten slightly. Cool for 30 minutes on a rack.
FOR GLAZE: Melt the chocolates and butter together in a heavy small saucepan over low heat, stirring until smooth. Let stand until cool enough to spread. Spread the glaze over the brownies. Let stand at room temperature for 4 hours before lifting the brownies from the pan using the foil sides and cutting into squares. (Can be prepared up to 1 day ahead. Store in an airtight container.)
